explicit_text: I was born in Bangkok, Thailand, and it is where my story begins. Growing up there, I lived in a Hmong village until 2002 when the International Organization for Migration came to interview us for resettlement. Bangkok is the place of my roots before I moved to the United States.

explicit_text: I arrived in the United States in 2004 when I was 13 years old, settling first in Denver, Colorado. The U.S. became my new home where I went to middle school and then Arvada High School. Playing soccer helped me learn English and connect with people from different backgrounds, which was essential for my adjustment and success here.

explicit_text: In 2010, my family and I moved from Colorado to Minnesota because we needed more help with food stamps, job opportunities, and to be closer to relatives. Life in Minnesota has been going well since then. I married my wife in 2011, have a part-time job at a casino, and continue my education, using the English I learned from playing soccer to communicate with my boss and teachers.
